name: Jersey

colors.symbolism: the gold of the Plantagenet crown and the lions of the Jersey arms

description: A red saltire on a white field, with the shield of Jersey (three gold lions passant guardant on red) surmounted by the gold Plantagenet crown placed at the centre.
history: The saltire on white field has been associated with Jersey since at least 1799, when it appears in a Dutch flag book. The current design, with the addition of the Plantagenet crown above the Jersey shield, was approved by Queen Elizabeth II in June 1979 and first hoisted on 7 April 1981. Before this revision the simple saltire alone was the de facto flag of the Bailiwick.

features.symbolism: the saltire is traditionally associated with Jersey, though its exact origin is debated; one theory identifies it as the cross of Saint Patrick, others as a 1783 admiralty error

three gold lions passant guardant on red — the historic arms of Jersey, derived from the royal arms of England in recognition of the island's allegiance to the English Crown since the loss of mainland Normandy in 1204
Jersey's status as a Crown Dependency, with the British monarch as the Duke of Normandy
